commit.message:
feat(cli): Kitty CSI u decoder + separator with git context (v0.16.0)

- Add CSI u decoder for Kitty keyboard protocol (fixes ALL Ctrl+key
  shortcuts in terminals using Kitty protocol like iTerm2)
- Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D, Ctrl+A/E, Ctrl+U/K/W now work in both legacy byte
  and CSI u encoding
- Add Ctrl+L (clear screen) for both legacy and CSI u
- Separator line now shows project name and git branch with change
  indicators: + (staged), * (unstaged), % (untracked)
- Git context refreshed each prompt turn

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: detect Python 3.13+ and auto-install 3.12 with venv

Python 3.13 is too new - torch, funasr, pystray don't have wheels yet.
Install script now:
- Detects 3.13+ and warns
- Auto-installs Python 3.12 via winget
- Creates venv with py -3.12
- README notes 3.10-3.12 requirement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Zoneless support (#6789)

* fix- daterange picker, default range in quick Select ranges

* refactor

* Initial plan

* Add comprehensive zoneless migration plan for ngx-bootstrap

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update zoneless migration plan per user feedback: breaking change, docs app, input/output migration

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 1: Enable zoneless change detection in docs app

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate alert, progressbar, accordion, collapse to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate rating component to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate tabs components to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2-3: Migrate carousel to zoneless (remove NgZone, use input/output signals)

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Disable husky pre-push hook for zoneless migration

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate buttons and sortable components to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate pagination components to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate dropdown and modal components to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate timepicker component to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: Migrate tooltip, popover, and typeahead to input()/output() signals

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 4: Update services for zoneless (remove NgZone from component-loader, positioning, focus-trap)

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 5-6: Complete migration - validate all tests, update documentation, re-enable husky

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 5-6: Complete migration - validate all tests, update documentation

Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor components to utilize input/output signals and improve state management

* Refactor accordion, carousel, and tabset components to use input bindings for configuration values instead of Object.assign

* Revert "Refactor accordion, carousel, and tabset components to use input bindings for configuration values instead of Object.assign"

This reverts commit 1cb23e0e28e114d1cfab92e02ab7bb6c212315a5.

* Refactor components to use configuration values from respective config objects instead of default values

* Refactor popover components to use configuration values from the config object

* Refactor ProgressbarComponent to use configuration values from ProgressbarConfig

* Cleanup ProgressbarComponent by removing unnecessary blank lines

* Refactor Timepicker and Tooltip components to use configuration values from respective config objects

* Fix memory leak by unsubscribing from positioning on component hide

* Enhance change detection in Typeahead components by marking for check on updates

* chore(deps): update Angular and Nx dependencies to latest versions

- Upgraded Angular packages from 20.0.2 to 21.1.1
- Upgraded Nx packages from 21.2.0 to 22.4.0
- Updated TypeScript from 5.8.2 to 5.9.3
- Updated ESLint packages to 8.53.1
- Updated ng-packagr to 21.0.0
- Added overrides for specific dependencies

* Revert "chore(deps): update Angular and Nx dependencies to latest versions"

This reverts commit 493e31020cfcd5eb1acf962ba97130da3c5b2032.

* Delete .vscode/settings.json

* final check before submit PR.

* fix linting issues

* Revert "fix- daterange picker, default range in quick Select ranges"

This reverts commit e357cdea8fc5dc8bb0e1720b07e475333b958a1e.

* fix: handle optional chaining for route data and update onPreview method signature

* refactor: remove zone change detection from bootstrap module and update event handling in dropdown demo

* refactor: remove unused NgIf, NgSwitch, NgSwitchCase, and NgFor imports from date range picker components

* fix(popover): convert to signals and fix zoneless compatibility

* fix(dropdown): add @Output decorators to events for zoneless compatibility

* fix(progressbar): add striped property to config so striped works from config

* fix(typeahead): default typeaheadHideResultsOnBlur to true so outside click closes dropdown

* fix(tabs): add ChangeDetectorRef.markForCheck for zoneless compatibility

* fix(carousel): only apply active input when explicitly bound to avoid override

* fix(pagination): use signal getters in template and add effect for rotate/maxSize

* fix(datepicker): simplify tooltip text reduce to filter for cleaner logic

* fix(docs): convert app sidebar to signal and fix modal demo initialState

* chore: update package-lock.json

* fix(dropdown): convert events from @Output EventEmitter to output() signals

* chore: update package-lock.json

* fix(dropdown): update test to call signal inputs instead of reading them as properties

* fix(timepicker): update tests to use signal inputs via setInput and call signal getters

* fix(rating): update test to use setInput for signal inputs

* fix(typeahead): update tests to use signal-compatible mocks and host component bindings

* fix(typeahead): revert typeaheadOptionsLimit default, align test with ?? 20 fallback and restore async detection test

* fix(pagination): update tests to use setInput for signal inputs

* fix(alert): update tests to use setInput for signal inputs and read internal _isOpen state

* fix(tabs): sync signal inputs in ngOnInit before addTab, update tests for signal reads

---------

Co-authored-by: Alexey Umanskiy <sharkakatsukie@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: DerekLiang <13641+DerekLiang@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Derek Liang <derek.liang@worksafebc.com>
Co-authored-by: Alex Umanskiy <aleksey.umanskiy@valor-software.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add rename symbol support (textDocument/rename)

- Add RenameEdit model (FilePath, Range, NewText)
- Add PrepareRenameAsync and RenameAsync to IClarionLanguageClient
- Add PrepareRename/Rename methods to LspClient calling textDocument/prepareRename
  and textDocument/rename LSP methods
- Add ParsePrepareRename and ParseRenameEdits parsers to ClarionLspService

The Node.js server (msarson.clarion-extensions) already supports rename —
this change exposes it through the C# wrapper interface.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: clarion-lsp
repository.description: Shared Clarion IDE addin — manages the Clarion Language Server (LSP) lifecycle and exposes IClarionLanguageClient for hover, definition, references, and symbol queries

commit.message:
fix: add --user to pip install for Microsoft Store Python compatibility

Microsoft Store Python installs to WindowsApps which is read-only.
pip install without --user silently fails on these installations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: channel-based exfil logger — eliminate concurrent write race

Replace shared *os.File with a buffered channel (cap 256) owned by a
single writer goroutine:

- Multiple handleConn goroutines call writeExfilLog concurrently;
  concurrent WriteString on the same *os.File is not safe and could
  produce interleaved or lost log entries.

- The writer goroutine is the sole owner of the file handle; it reads
  from exfilCh and writes entries sequentially, surfacing any write
  errors to stderr rather than silently discarding them.

- On SIGTERM/SIGINT the accept loop exits, exfilCh is closed, and
  writerDone.Wait() ensures all queued detection events are flushed
  before the process exits.

- writeExfilLog uses a non-blocking select/default so a full channel
  (e.g. during an unexpected burst) logs a warning to stderr rather
  than blocking the ICAP handler goroutine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: smoke tests — expired CA cert reuse and grep -c bug in test 15

- smoke.sh: validate CA cert expiry before reusing (openssl -checkend 3600);
  if the cert exists but has expired (e.g. a 1-day test cert from an interrupted
  run), remove it and regenerate. Prevents ssl_bump tests failing with
  'certificate has expired' on the next run.

- smoke.sh: fix grep -c zero-match bug in test 15 EXFIL_ENTRY check;
  use 'sh -c "grep -c ...; true"' pattern (same as test 16) so the
  subshell exit code is always 0 and the integer comparison is valid.

commit.message:
Fix config design: user config is now override-only template

Previously install.sh copied defaults.cfg as the user config, so all
default values were 'frozen' in ~/.config/vox-linux/config.cfg. Any
future change to defaults.cfg was silently ignored for existing users.

New design:
- config/defaults.cfg       — built-in defaults, always sourced first
- config/config.cfg.template — all options commented out; copied to
                               ~/.config/vox-linux/config.cfg on install
- User config holds only explicit overrides; everything else falls
  through to defaults automatically

install.sh updated to copy the template instead of defaults.cfg.
Existing user config replaced with the clean template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool (#4609)

* Initial plan

* feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool

Implements roadmap item #219 (v1.9.0):

- Remove per-invocation cudaMalloc/cudaFree from launchHnswSearchKernel();
  caller now provides pre-allocated d_visited pool (nullptr → overflow signal)
- Add persistent visited bitset pool to CudaHnswTraversalEngine::Impl:
  allocated once in buildIndex() at maxBatchSize × ceil(numNodes/8) bytes,
  freed in freeDevice()
- Implement chunked batch processing in batchSearch(): when pool_capacity <
  num_queries, split into sub-batches of pool_capacity, process serially,
  concatenate on host. Covers both single-pass and multi-pass (large-k) paths.
- Expose CudaHnswTraversalEngine::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n) and
  CUDAVectorBackend::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n)
- Clamp pool to 90% of BackendCapabilities::maxMemoryBytes in buildHnswAnnIndex()
- Set BackendHealthStatus::makeDegraded() via setError() when pool allocation
  fails so getHealthStatus() surfaces the issue to callers
- Add 14 new tests: setMaxBatchSize, hasVisitedPool, chunked correctness,
  degraded health on pool failure
- Update FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md and ROADMAP.md to mark item #219 done

commit.message:
feat: auto-calculate tool count + ROI pricing from TOML

P0 automation fixes:
- tool_count now auto-calculated from toolkit_nav.toml (was hardcoded 26)
- Never needs manual bumping when adding/removing tools
- ROI Calculator pricing now reads from data/roi_pricing.toml via Hugo
- JS falls back to hardcoded values if TOML injection fails
- Removed tool_count from hugo.toml (no longer needed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

PR Reviewer: auto-fix staging, auto-generate, history restore

Git-style auto-fix workflow:
- 'Fix' button generates a code fix via Copilot CLI and stages it
- Staged fixes panel shows colored diff previews
- 'Unstage' removes a fix, 'Commit All Fixes' pushes to PR branch
- Fix buttons only appear on your own PRs (author detection via ADO API)

Auto-generate review:
- Loading a PR now automatically generates AI review (no extra click)

History restore:
- 'Restore' button on history entries reloads the PR with saved comments
- Restored reviews have full Post/Fix/Delete capabilities

ADO client additions:
- get_current_user() for author detection
- get_file_at_branch() for fetching source files
- get_branch_head() + push_file_changes() for committing fixes
- author_id stored in pr_info

AI reviewer additions:
- generate_fix() uses Copilot CLI to produce fixed file content
- Strips markdown fences from fix output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Discordニックネーム二重フォーマットバグを修正

Bot APIが独自にニックネームのフォーマット(学籍番号付与等)を行うため、
formatDiscordNicknameで事前フォーマットした名前を送ると
「名前(学籍番号)(学籍番号)」のように二重付与されていた。

全5箇所のsyncDiscordNickname呼び出しを素の名前(姓+名)に変更し、
不要になったformatDiscordNickname関数を削除。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix suggest command: gh copilot suggest → gh copilot -i

'gh copilot suggest "query"' is no longer the correct format.
The working command is 'gh copilot -i "query"'.

Updated both the default in config/defaults.cfg and the fallback
in lib/type.sh. Users can still override via VOX_SUGGEST_CMD in
their config.
